text: The verb used in the Hebrew text can be translated ‘I will be’, and it is possible to understand the formula as meaning, ‘I will be what I will be’. In the second half of the verse, according to the Hebrew text, the name used is ‘I am’ (or, ‘I will be’), rather than ‘He who is’. But the personal name under which Almighty God was known to the Jews was Yahweh, He who is. The Greek translators, out of reverence, substituted ‘the Lord’ wherever this name occurred in the Old Testament, and the Latin follows them. Thus, in verse 15 immediately following, ‘Yahweh the God of their fathers’ appears in the Latin as ‘the Lord, the God of their fathers’.
